overview: Esper Lurrus Control leverages cheap, disruptive creatures to stall aggressive strategies and dismantle combo plans in Vintage. By cycling Lurrus of the Dream-Den, the deck generates massive card advantage while recycling key threats and removal. Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters provide early tempo and information, allowing you to dictate the pace until your superior resource generation closes out the game.
win_conditions: Recycling Lurrus and cheap threats to outvalue opponents over multiple turns
Using Psychic Frog to reveal and disrupt critical combo pieces or win conditions
Deploying Orcish Bowmasters to remove key threats while maintaining board presence

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and a one-drop creature
Combinations featuring Lurrus alongside removal or disruption
Draws containing Psychic Frog or Orcish Bowmasters with early interaction
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetch/shock if applicable), early interaction (Thoughtseize, Inquisition, Fatal Push, Spell Pierce), and Lurrus or Psychic Frog.
mulligan_criteria: Mana screws lacking early land drops
Hands heavy on late-game cards without early tempo
Draws missing interaction against the current meta
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only 3+ mana spells, or hands with no early interaction against aggro.

early_game: Prioritize casting one-drops to establish tempo and gather information
Use Psychic Frog to force reveals and adjust your game plan accordingly
Play lands and cheap disruption to stabilize against fast strategies
Turn 1: Play land, cast Thoughtseize/Inquisition if possible, or hold for Lurrus/interaction.
Turn 2: Lurrus, Psychic Frog, or interaction.
Sequence lands carefully (fetch/shock to duals, then basics).
Prioritize finding duals for Esper colors.
mid_game: Begin cycling Lurrus to return creatures from the graveyard to your hand
Trade Orcish Bowmasters for high-value threats to maintain board control
Filter your deck with cheap spells to find answers to opponent's threats
Use Lurrus to cast 2-drops multiple times per turn.
avoid overloading it if facing graveyard hate, but leverage it for the Lurrus engine.
Preserve card advantage by trading efficiently and using cheap interaction.
Commit threats when you have removal for their threats or when they lack sweepers.
Hold back if they have board wipes or heavy interaction.
late_game: Leverage infinite Lurrus recursion loops to exhaust opponent's resources
Recycle Orcish Bowmasters repeatedly to clear the board and close the game
Maintain card advantage by continuously cycling cheap creatures and interaction
Lurrus engine, Psychic Frog, Orcish Bowmasters, and Teferi's Protection-protected threats.
Close out by recurring 2-drops, trading efficiently, and applying pressure when the opponent lacks answers.
Go all-in when the opponent has no sweepers, low life total, or you have decisive card advantage.
Commit when you can safely cast Lurrus or Psychic Frog and follow up with recurring threats.

key_cards.name: Lurrus of the Dream-Den
key_cards.role: Core recursion engine and card advantage generator
key_cards.notes: Cast early, then repeatedly return it from the graveyard to your hand to pay its cost and cycle other cheap creatures or removal spells.

key_cards.name: Orcish Bowmasters
key_cards.notes: Use its death trigger to destroy opponent's key creatures, then recycle it with Lurrus to generate continuous value and board presence.

key_cards.name: Psychic Frog
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to force an opponent to reveal a card, helping you identify combo pieces or adjust your sequencing against their strategy.

sequencing_tips: Always cast Psychic Frog before playing other creatures to maximize information gain
Hold up Lurrus recursion until you have a clear target in the graveyard to cycle
Use Orcish Bowmasters' death trigger to remove threats that would otherwise trigger opponent's abilities
Prioritize land drops early to ensure you can pay Lurrus's recursion cost consistently
protect it with removal or Teferi's Protection.
Don't overcommit to the graveyard if facing hate.
Psychic Frog is a win condition but vulnerable to sweepers.
Teferi's Protection is versatile but limited
use it on key spells or threats.

matchups.advice: Focus on disrupting their key pieces with Psychic Frog and use Lurrus to recycle interaction until they run out of resources.
Play cheap creatures early to stall and use Orcish Bowmasters to remove their biggest threats while recycling Lurrus for value.
Race their resource generation by cycling Lurrus aggressively and using Orcish Bowmasters to clear their board before they stabilize.

Use cheap spells, split casts, or threats that are hard to counter (Lurrus, Psychic Frog). Use Teferi's Protection to protect key spells. Apply pressure to force them to use counters on non-critical spells.
Early interaction, Thoughtseize to hit mana dorks, removal for key threats, and Teferi's Protection to protect your game plan. Stabilize quickly and out-value them in the late game.
Focus on card advantage, better sequencing, and sideboard hate. Use Teferi's Protection to protect key spells, Surgical Extraction to disrupt their graveyard, and out-value them in extended turns.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath, Anger, etc.), counterspells, and graveyard strategies.
Note when opponents tap out for sweepers or hold up interaction.
Track graveyard contents if facing Dredge or reanimation.
